SEARCH
Package server.dao

Class ReservationDAO

java.lang.Object
server.dao.ReservationDAO
public class ReservationDAO extends Object
Data access object for the reservation table.

Follows the project's standard DAO conventions: each method opens a short-lived Connection from DBConnection, runs parameterized statements, and maps rows to ReservationDTO. SQL exceptions are logged and surfaced as a null/false/-1 return rather than being propagated, so callers signal failure through the return value.

    int
    availableCapacity(int parkId, String visitDate)
    Computes the remaining headroom for a park on a given date: the park's max_capacity minus its gap_size minus the total party size of all PENDING/CONFIRMED reservations for that park and date.
    ReservationDTO
    findByConfirmationCode(int confirmationCode)
    Looks up a single reservation by its booking confirmation code — the lookup the gate performs at entry, where the visitor presents the code printed on their booking.

    Lists every reservation owned by a visitor, most recent visit first.

    Finds the PENDING reservations whose confirmation window has lapsed: still PENDING, already reminded (reminder_sent_at IS NOT NULL), and reminded more than timeoutMinutes ago.

    Finds the reservations that the no-show sweep should mark ReservationStatus.NO_SHOW: those still CONFIRMED whose visit_date is strictly before today (a same-day booking can still arrive) and for which no visit row was ever recorded — i.e.
    List<ReservationDTO>

    Force-aware variant of findNoShowCandidates().
    List<ReservationDTO>

    Finds the PENDING reservations due a confirmation reminder: those whose visit (combining visit_date with visit_time, or midnight when the time is null) falls between now and leadHours from now, and that have not already been reminded (reminder_sent_at IS NULL).

  • Constructor Details

    • ReservationDAO

      public ReservationDAO()

  • Method Details

    • getById

      public ReservationDTO getById(int id)
      Looks up a single reservation by its primary key.
      Parameters:
      id - the reservation identifier to fetch
      Returns:
      the matching ReservationDTO, or null if no row matches or the query fails

    • findByConfirmationCode

      public ReservationDTO findByConfirmationCode(int confirmationCode)
      Looks up a single reservation by its booking confirmation code — the lookup the gate performs at entry, where the visitor presents the code printed on their booking. Confirmation codes are not unique by schema; on the (rare) chance two reservations share a code, the most recent row wins.
      Parameters:
      confirmationCode - the booking confirmation code to match
      Returns:
      the matching ReservationDTO, or null if none matches or the query fails

    • findByVisitor

      public List<ReservationDTO> findByVisitor(long visitorId)
      Lists every reservation owned by a visitor, most recent visit first.
      Parameters:
      visitorId - the visitor whose reservations to fetch
      Returns:
      the visitor's reservations (possibly empty); never null

    • insert

      public int insert(ReservationDTO r)
      Inserts a new reservation, letting the database assign the auto-increment id.
      Parameters:
      r - the reservation to persist (its id and createdAt are ignored)
      Returns:
      the new auto-generated id, or -1 if the insert fails

    • updateStatus

      public boolean updateStatus(int id, ReservationStatus status)
      Updates the lifecycle status of a reservation and stamps status_changed_at = NOW() in the same statement.

      This is the single choke point for every status transition (confirm, cancel, accept-grab, exit→completed, no-show), so the timestamp is recorded in one place rather than at each call site. The stamp backs the Cancellations Report's "when was it cancelled" column.

      Parameters:
      id - the reservation to update
      status - the new status
      Returns:
      true if a row was updated, false otherwise

    • updateReschedule

      public boolean updateReschedule(int id, String visitDate, String visitTime, int partySize, int priceCents)
      Reschedules a reservation: updates the scheduling fields (date, time, party size) and the recomputed price in the same statement.

      The price is written here because changing the party size changes what the party owes; persisting it alongside the schedule keeps the row's price_cents consistent with its party_size instead of leaving a stale price behind. The caller computes the new price (via PricingService) and passes it in.

      Parameters:
      id - the reservation to update
      visitDate - the new visit date, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      visitTime - the new visit time (HH:mm:ss), or null to clear it
      partySize - the new party size
      priceCents - the recomputed price for the new party size, in cents
      Returns:
      true if a row was updated, false otherwise

    • findNoShowCandidates

      public List<ReservationDTO> findNoShowCandidates()
      Finds the reservations that the no-show sweep should mark ReservationStatus.NO_SHOW: those still CONFIRMED whose visit_date is strictly before today (a same-day booking can still arrive) and for which no visit row was ever recorded — i.e. the party never entered the park. Ordered by date for a stable sweep.

      Equivalent to findNoShowCandidates(false).

      Returns:
      the no-show candidates (possibly empty); never null

    • findNoShowCandidates

      public List<ReservationDTO> findNoShowCandidates(boolean force)
      Force-aware variant of findNoShowCandidates(). With force the strictly-before-today bound is relaxed to visit_date <= CURDATE() so a CONFIRMED booking for today with no entry is also marked — this is the manual "run now" path, letting the live defense produce a no-show on a same-day seeded reservation without waiting for the date to roll over.
      Parameters:
      force - true to include today's date; false for the normal strictly-past sweep
      Returns:
      the no-show candidates (possibly empty); never null

    • findReminderCandidates

      public List<ReservationDTO> findReminderCandidates(int leadHours)
      Finds the PENDING reservations due a confirmation reminder: those whose visit (combining visit_date with visit_time, or midnight when the time is null) falls between now and leadHours from now, and that have not already been reminded (reminder_sent_at IS NULL). The null guard is what stops the reminder being re-sent on every poll. Past visits are excluded — a reminder to confirm a visit that has already happened is moot.

      Equivalent to findReminderCandidates(leadHours, false).

      Parameters:
      leadHours - how far ahead of the visit a reminder should fire
      Returns:
      the reservations due a reminder (possibly empty); never null

    • findReminderCandidates

      public List<ReservationDTO> findReminderCandidates(int leadHours, boolean force)
      Force-aware variant of findReminderCandidates(int). With force the upper leadHours bound is dropped, so every upcoming un-reminded PENDING reservation is returned regardless of how far out it is — the manual "run now" path, which lets the live defense fire a reminder immediately without a 24h-out booking. The >= NOW() (upcoming) and reminder_sent_at IS NULL (not-yet-reminded) guards are kept either way: a reminder for a past visit is moot, and the null guard is what stops a re-send on the next poll.
      Parameters:
      leadHours - how far ahead of the visit a reminder should fire (ignored when force)
      force - true to drop the lead-hours window; false for the normal sweep
      Returns:
      the reservations due a reminder (possibly empty); never null

    • markReminderSent

      public boolean markReminderSent(int id)
      Stamps reminder_sent_at = NOW() on a reservation, marking that its confirmation reminder has gone out so findReminderCandidates(int) will skip it on the next poll.
      Parameters:
      id - the reservation that was just reminded
      Returns:
      true if a row was updated, false otherwise

    • findConfirmTimeoutCandidates

      public List<ReservationDTO> findConfirmTimeoutCandidates(int timeoutMinutes)
      Finds the PENDING reservations whose confirmation window has lapsed: still PENDING, already reminded (reminder_sent_at IS NOT NULL), and reminded more than timeoutMinutes ago. The status = 'PENDING' filter is what makes confirming in time safe — once a visitor confirms (PENDING→CONFIRMED) the row drops out of this result and is never cancelled.

      Equivalent to findConfirmTimeoutCandidates(timeoutMinutes, false).

      Parameters:
      timeoutMinutes - the confirmation window length, in minutes
      Returns:
      the reservations to auto-cancel (possibly empty); never null

    • findConfirmTimeoutCandidates

      public List<ReservationDTO> findConfirmTimeoutCandidates(int timeoutMinutes, boolean force)
      Force-aware variant of findConfirmTimeoutCandidates(int). With force both the reminder_sent_at IS NOT NULL guard and the timeout-window predicate are dropped, so every still-PENDING reservation is returned for cancellation now — the manual "run now" path, which lets the live defense produce an auto-cancel immediately without first sending a reminder or waiting out the confirmation window. The status = 'PENDING' filter is always kept, so a reservation a visitor already confirmed is never cancelled.
      Parameters:
      timeoutMinutes - the confirmation window length, in minutes (ignored when force)
      force - true to cancel every PENDING now; false for the normal window sweep
      Returns:
      the reservations to auto-cancel (possibly empty); never null

    • availableCapacity

      public int availableCapacity(int parkId, String visitDate)
      Computes the remaining headroom for a park on a given date: the park's max_capacity minus its gap_size minus the total party size of all PENDING/CONFIRMED reservations for that park and date.
      Parameters:
      parkId - the park to check
      visitDate - the visit date to check, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      Returns:
      the remaining capacity (may be negative if overbooked), or -1 if the park is unknown or the query fails

    • guideExists

      public boolean guideExists(long visitorId)
      Checks whether a registered guide exists with the given visitor id.
      Parameters:
      visitorId - the candidate guide's visitor (national) id
      Returns:
      true if a matching row exists in the guide table, false if none matches or the query fails

    • map

      private ReservationDTO map(ResultSet rs) throws SQLException
      Maps the current row of a ResultSet to a ReservationDTO.

      Date/time columns are read with getString (matching the DTO's String-date convention and tolerating a NULL visit_time). The enum columns are parsed with valueOf, and the nullable guide_id/confirmation_code columns use wasNull() so a SQL NULL becomes a Java null instead of a zero.

      Parameters:
      rs - a result set positioned on a reservation row
      Returns:
      the mapped reservation
      Throws:
      SQLException - if a column cannot be read
